_description: Road maintenance responsibility patterns by state. Used to set intelligent default road type filters when a user selects a jurisdiction. Based on FHWA Highway Statistics, state DOT documentation, and Byrd Road Act (VA), NCDOT system (NC), etc.

state_maintained.description: State DOT maintains most secondary/local roads (53-89%). Counties have little/no road responsibility.

county_maintained.description: Counties maintain their own local road networks. State DOT only handles highways/interstates. This is the dominant national pattern (~35 states).

town_maintained.description: Towns/townships are the primary road-maintaining entity (New England model). County government abolished or vestigial.

mixed.description: Varies significantly by jurisdiction within the state. Requires per-jurisdiction overrides.

single_entity.description: Single jurisdiction with no county/city distinction. All roads maintained by one entity.

VA._notes: VDOT maintains ~80% of roads (Byrd Road Act 1932). Arlington & Henrico counties maintain their own roads. All 38 independent cities maintain their own streets.

MD._notes: Baltimore City maintains ALL roads within city limits, including interstates. Montgomery County maintains its own roads. Other counties vary.

CO._notes: Denver is a consolidated city-county maintaining all its own roads.

CA._notes: San Francisco is a consolidated city-county.

KY._notes: Louisville-Jefferson and Lexington-Fayette are consolidated city-counties.

MO._notes: St. Louis City is independent from St. Louis County (separated 1876).

NV._notes: Carson City is a consolidated city-county.

GA._notes: Athens-Clarke, Augusta-Richmond, Columbus-Muscogee, Macon-Bibb are consolidated.
